title: Isolation of temperate phages by plaque agar overlay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3n4lt","text":"This protocol gives a method for isolating temperate phages from marine viral concentrations. This protocol uses the conventional plaque agar overlay and looking for turbid or haloed plaques, a hallmark of temperate phages.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3u403","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bjpd5","text":"Paul, J. H., and M. Weinbauer. 2010. Detection of lysogeny in marine environments, p. 30–33. In S. W. Wilhelm, M. G. Weinbauer, and C. A. Suttle [eds.], Manual of Aquatic Viral Ecology. ASLO.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9c9ja","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"124em","text":"Please see the full chapter for additional details.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":15,"length":12}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"89A20A1EB18740AE9C52858FB3C5E715","url":"http://www.aslo.org/books/mave/MAVE_030.pdf"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"nfdk","text":"Overview","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"239ch","text":"The isolation of temperate phages requires a cultivatable host and a source of concentrated viruses. For example, we isolated a pseudotemperate phage (φHSIC) and its host from the same bacterial/viral concentrate (Jiang et al. 1998) that we obtained in the Sand Island Channel, Oahu, HI, USA. The host was first isolated by standard isolation streaking on marine agar using inoculum from a microbial population concentrate. This protocol uses the conventional plaque agar overlay and looking for turbid or haloed plaques, a hallmark of temperate phages.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2cmfm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9bukp","text":"Materials","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8hpll","text":"Standard marine agar (1.5%) plates (i.e., Zobell 2216 or ASWJP);","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bqiee","text":"Sterile marine broth; ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c0nj6","text":"Sterile marine soft agar (1%), 3 mL per 15-mL tube; ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a3ojb","text":"Water bath;  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dlsba","text":"Marine host bacterial culture in exponential growth, 20–50 mL; ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"64jjd","text":"Viral concentrate.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Soft agar overlay tubes are melted in boiling water and placed in the 47°C water bath.
One tube of soft agar is removed from the water bath.
1.0 mL host culture and either 1.0 or 0.1 mL viral concentrate is added.
The contents of the tube is mixed well by rolling back and forth between two hands, and the tube contents are immediately emptied onto an agar plate.
The top agar is gently spread over the agar surface by sliding the plate on the bench surface using a circular motion.
The top agar is allowed to harden by not disturbing the plates for 30 min. 00:30:00
The plates are incubated (top agar side down) overnight to 48 h. 48:00:00
Temperate phage plaques will appear as turbid or cloudy plaques, whereas purely lytic phage will appear as sharply defined, clear plaques.
authors: John H. Paul and Markus Weinbauer
creator: Verve Team
